title: Sunday Trinket Box
summary: A small hinge-free trinket box with a floating inner lid, built from a single craft board or cedar picket.
description:
This box skips hinges entirely — the inner lid simply sits inside the opening, trimmed about 1/8" smaller than the box's inner dimensions so it lifts out cleanly without catching on the sides. That small clearance is worth getting right: too tight and the lid binds, too loose and it rattles.

The whole box comes from one board, whether that's a craft-store board or a cedar picket — if your stock doesn't measure the standard 5-1/2" x 1/2", the side length is just your board's width minus twice its thickness, and the inner lid is cut 1/8" smaller than that. Finish before attaching the inner lid, and choose wood filler if you're painting or color-matched wood putty if you're staining, since the two fillers behave very differently under a clear finish.

materials.name: 1/2" x 5-1/2" craft board, 2 ft (or a 1/2" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft cedar picket)
1/2" x 2-1/2" craft board, 3 ft
materials.note: Not needed if using a cedar picket for everything.

materials.name: Super glue
materials.note: Only needed if using a wood pull instead of a store-bought knob.

materials.name: Wood putty, color-matched
materials.note: Use if staining or finishing clear.

materials.name: Knob or pull
materials.note: Optional — skip if making a wood pull from scrap instead.

cutList.note: Trim to fit — about 1/8" smaller than the box's inner dimensions on each side.

steps.title: Assemble the box sides
steps.body: Attach the sides between the front and back with 1" finish nails and glue, keeping the tops, bottoms, and outside edges flush.
materials: 1/2" x 5-1/2" craft board, 2 ft (or a 1/2" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft cedar picket)

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Attach the bottom to the box, keeping the outside edges flush, using finish nails and glue.
materials: 1/2" x 5-1/2" craft board, 2 ft (or a 1/2" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft cedar picket)

steps.title: Cut the inner lid
steps.body: Cut the inner lid and trim it to fit — it should sit inside the box easily without catching on the sides. Hold off on attaching it until after finishing.
materials: 1/2" x 5-1/2" craft board, 2 ft (or a 1/2" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft cedar picket)

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand and finish as desired. If painting, use wood filler on the nail holes first. If staining or finishing clear, finish the box first, then fill any nail holes afterward with color-matched wood putty.

steps.title: Attach the pull and set the inner lid in place
steps.body: Attach the knob or wood pull to the top of the box (super glue works well for a wood pull). Set the finished inner lid inside the box — it rests in place rather than being fastened down, so it lifts out freely.

images.alt: Small wooden trinket box with a floating inner lid
